title: Glazed Algerian Tuna Tagine
description: A nutty, silky, and adventurous Algerian tagine that marries smoky tuna with a spicy madras paste and caramelized cashew lardons for a pescatarian feast.
instructions: Heat a tagine or heavy-bottomed pot over medium heat and drizzle 1 tbsp olive oil.
Add the thinly sliced onion and sauté until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic and madras paste, cooking for 2 minutes until fragrant.
Pour in the tomato puree and canned chickpeas, mixing well, and bring the mixture to a gentle simmer.
Insert the tuna pieces, spooning sauce over them, and cover; let cook for 6 minutes until the tuna is just opaque.
While the tuna simmers, heat the remaining 1 tbsp oil in a small pan, add the roasted cashew lardons, and toast until golden, about 3 minutes.
Once the tuna is ready, fold in the toasted cashew lardons and drizzle lemon juice, seasoning with salt and pepper to taste.
Prepare couscous according to package instructions, fluffing with a fork, and serve the tagine over a bed of couscous.
Garnish with chopped cilantro and a sprinkle of extra cashew lardons before serving.
